mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE), a managed Kubernetes service on Oracle Cloud. It is ideal for users looking to deploy Kubernetes clusters without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Oracle's cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the OCI CLI
steps.description:
The OCI CLI is required to interact with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. This step installs the CLI on your local machine.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the OCI CLI.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster in Oracle Cloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..your_compartment_id --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.1 --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..your_vcn_id --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

```

> Replace 'your_compartment_id' and 'your_vcn_id' with your actual OCIDs.
commands: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id ocid1.compartment.oc1..your_compartment_id --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.1 --vcn-id ocid1.vcn.oc1..your_vcn_id --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to communicate with your new cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --file $HOME/.kube/config --region us-phoenix-1 --token-version 2.0

```

> Replace 'your_cluster_id' with your actual cluster OCID.
commands: oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --file $HOME/.kube/config --region us-phoenix-1 --token-version 2.0

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster, as well as RBAC for security.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/examples/rbac.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/examples/autoscaler.yaml

```

> This command applies the RBAC configuration.
> This command sets up the autoscaler.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --force

```

> Replace 'your_cluster_id' with your actual cluster OCID.

uninstall.title: Remove Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command helps to clean up any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

oci lb load-balancer delete --load-balancer-id ocid1.loadbalancer.oc1..your_load_balancer_id --force

```

> Replace 'your_load_balancer_id' with your actual load balancer OCID.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and drain the nodes. This command upgrades your cluster to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --kubernetes-version v1.30.2

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Replace '<node-name>' with the name of your node.
> Replace 'your_cluster_id' with your actual cluster OCID.
> Replace '<node-name>' with the name of your node to allow scheduling.
commands: oci ce cluster update --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --kubernetes-version v1.30.2

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig file or the cluster's certificate.

**Fix:**
Regenerate the kubeconfig file using the correct cluster ID.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that no resources have been deployed in the default namespace.

**Fix:**
Deploy a sample application to verify the cluster is functioning.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for scheduling.

**Fix:**
Scale up the node pool or adjust resource requests for your deployments.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the VCN or subnets are misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the VCN and subnet configurations and ensure they are set up correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new OCI services, improved integration with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id ocid1.cluster.oc1..your_cluster_id --file $HOME/.kube/config --region us-phoenix-1 --token-version 2.0

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the OCI CLI installed and configured with your Oracle Cloud credentials.
